workflows.title: Minimum degree three gives an even cycle

stages.title: Endpoint-parity problem
stages.summary: The graph profile supplies the minimum-degree baseline and even-cycle target used by every downstream stage.

primaryDeclaration.type: (V : Type u) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EndpointParityCycle.Profile V
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.endpointParityProfile

evidenceDeclarations.type: {V : Type u} → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V → Prop
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.Baseline

evidenceDeclarations.type: {V : Type u} → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V → Prop
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.HasEvenCycle

stages.title: CT6 maximal-path ledger
stages.summary: CT6 searches the chosen maximal path and returns the active-ledger residual at its endpoint.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (baseline : Baseline object),
  StructuralExhaustion.CT6.ActiveLedgerRun (endpointParityProfile V).ct6Spec
    ((endpointParityProfile V).ct6Capability object) ((endpointParityProfile V).branchContext object baseline)
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.ct6Run

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (baseline : Baseline object) (vertex : V),
  ¬(ct6Spec V).Failure (branchContext object baseline) vertex
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ct6_no_failure

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (baseline : Baseline object) (vertex : V),
  object.graph.Adj (chosenMaximalPath object baseline).snd.path.endpoint vertex →
    vertex ∈ (chosenMaximalPath object baseline).snd.path.vertices
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ct6_endpoint_closed

evidenceDeclarations.type: ConcreteK4.ct6Execution.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Graph.Terminal.activeLedger
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.ct6_terminal_activeLedger

evidenceDeclarations.type:
StructuralExhaustion.CT6.ExecutionResult.trace (endpointParityProfile ConcreteK4.Vertex).ct6Spec
    ((endpointParityProfile ConcreteK4.Vertex).ct6Capability ConcreteK4.object)
    ((endpointParityProfile ConcreteK4.Vertex).branchContext ConcreteK4.object ConcreteK4.minimumDegreeThree)
    ConcreteK4.ct6Execution =
  [StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Graph.NodeId.firstFailureSearch,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Graph.NodeId.activeLedgerTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.ct6_trace_exact

stages.title: CT9 parity overload
stages.summary: The registered route converts endpoint-neighbor positions into a capacity-one parity instance for CT9.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (baseline : Baseline object),
  StructuralExhaustion.CT9.ParityCapacityOneRun ((endpointParityProfile V).endpointRank object baseline)
    ((endpointParityProfile V).ct9Input object baseline)
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.ct9Run

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (baseline : Baseline object) →
      StructuralExhaustion.Routes.CT6ToCT9.ItemCollectionAdapter
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT6.ActiveLedgerResidual (endpointParityProfile V).ct6Spec
          ((endpointParityProfile V).ct6Capability object) ((endpointParityProfile V).branchContext object baseline))
        ((endpointParityProfile V).ct9Capability object baseline).Item
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ct6ToCT9ItemAdapter

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (baseline : Baseline object),
  3 ≤ (ct9Input object baseline).items.values.length
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ct9_three_le_item_cardinality

evidenceDeclarations.type: ConcreteK4.ct9Execution.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT9.Graph.Terminal.overloaded
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.ct9_terminal_overloaded

evidenceDeclarations.type:
StructuralExhaustion.CT9.ExecutionResult.trace
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT9.parityCapacityOne (endpointParityProfile ConcreteK4.Vertex).base.problem ConcreteK4.Vertex
      ((endpointParityProfile ConcreteK4.Vertex).endpointRank ConcreteK4.object ConcreteK4.minimumDegreeThree))
    ((endpointParityProfile ConcreteK4.Vertex).ct9Input ConcreteK4.object ConcreteK4.minimumDegreeThree)
    ConcreteK4.ct9Execution =
  [StructuralExhaustion.CT9.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT9.Graph.NodeId.partition,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT9.Graph.NodeId.overload, StructuralExhaustion.CT9.Graph.NodeId.overloadedTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.ct9_trace_exact

stages.title: Even-cycle certificate
stages.summary: Equal-parity endpoint positions determine a chord cycle with certified even length.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    Baseline object → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength object.graph fun length => length % 2 = 0
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.evenCycleCertificate

evidenceDeclarations.type:
ConcreteK4.evenCycle.walk.support =
  [ConcreteK4.Vertex.v3, ConcreteK4.Vertex.v2, ConcreteK4.Vertex.v1, ConcreteK4.Vertex.v0, ConcreteK4.Vertex.v3]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.extracted_even_cycle_support

evidenceDeclarations.type: ConcreteK4.evenCycle.walk.length = 4
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.extracted_even_cycle_length

stages.title: CT1 certificate validation
stages.summary: CT1 validates the already constructed cycle against the finite target encoding.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (baseline : Baseline object) →
      StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedC1Run (endpointParityProfile V).base.ct1Spec
        ((endpointParityProfile V).ct1Input object baseline)
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.finalCT1Run

evidenceDeclarations.type: ConcreteK4.ct1Run.result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.c1
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.ct1_terminal_c1

evidenceDeclarations.type:
ConcreteK4.ct1Run.result.trace =
  [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.c1Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK4.ct1_trace_exact

stages.title: Even-cycle theorem
stages.summary: The public theorem concludes that every finite graph of minimum degree at least three has an even simple cycle.

primaryDeclaration.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V), Baseline object → HasEvenCycle object
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.minimumDegreeThree_hasEvenCycle

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V), Baseline object → HasEvenCycle object
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.hasEvenCycle_of_minDegree_three

workflows.purpose: Follow the complete CT6→CT9 construction, its cycle certificate, and the independent CT1 validation lane.

links.label: instantiates CT6
links.description: The endpoint-parity profile generates the CT6 specification, capability, and maximal-path input.

links.label: active-ledger route

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (baseline : Baseline object) →
      StructuralExhaustion.Routes.CT6ToCT9.ItemCollectionAdapter
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT6.ActiveLedgerResidual (endpointParityProfile V).ct6Spec
          ((endpointParityProfile V).ct6Capability object) ((endpointParityProfile V).branchContext object baseline))
        ((endpointParityProfile V).ct9Capability object baseline).Item
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ct6ToCT9ItemAdapter

links.description: The framework-registered CT6 active-ledger residual route constructs the CT9 input through the problem adapter.

links.label: overloaded parity fibre

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (baseline : Baseline object) →
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EndpointParityCycle.SameParityEndpointPositions
        ((endpointParityProfile V).activePath object baseline)
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.sameParityEndpointPositions

links.description: CT9's overloaded fibre supplies two endpoint-neighbor positions of the same parity.

links.label: witnesses target

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V), Baseline object → HasEvenCycle object
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.hasEvenCycle_of_minDegree_three

links.description: The certified chord cycle is the witness consumed by the target theorem.

links.label: validated by CT1
links.description: CT1 checks the public certificate; it does not create the CT6→CT9 witness.

links.label: target confirmed
links.description: The successful CT1 run independently confirms that the constructed witness realizes the target.

workflows.title: Deletion-critical CT2 audit

stages.title: Minimal-counterexample context
stages.summary: The same even-cycle problem is viewed through its generated deletion-only CT2 profile.

primaryDeclaration.type: ∀ (V : Type u), StructuralExhaustion.CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule (staticInput V).ct2Capability
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.deletionClosureRule

evidenceDeclarations.type: (V : Type u) → StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem (staticInput V).problem
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.pieces

stages.title: CT2 no proper core
stages.summary: The certificate-driven CT2 profile checks one proper finite subgraph and proves that it cannot retain minimum degree three.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V),
  Baseline object →
    ¬HasEvenCycle object →
      ∃ ctx,
        CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.problem.rank ctx.G ≤
            CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.problem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.EdgeRootedNoProperCorePrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.exists_noProperCorePrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.problem
      CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (CT2Audit.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).terminal =
    StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.properCoreCT2Run_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.problem
      CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (CT2Audit.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).trace =
    [StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.properCoreCT2Run_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.problem
      CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (CT2Audit.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).checks = 1
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.properCoreCT2Run_checks

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.problem
      CT2Audit.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G), subgraph.value.object.minDegree ≤ 2
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.properSubgraph_minDegree_le_two

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  (input.properSubgraphCT2Run ctx subgraph baseline).checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).size
            (input.properSubgraphCT2Input ctx subgraph baseline) +
          1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).degree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run_polynomial

stages.title: CT2 heavy-dart run
stages.summary: CT2 exhausts deletion and replacement for an explicitly exhibited heavy dart.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (problem V) HasEvenCycle) →
    (dart : ctx.G.graph.Dart) →
      (heavy :
          4 ≤ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.1 ∧
            4 ≤ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.2) →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT2.LocalDeletionRun (staticInput V).ct2Capability ctx
          { seed := { piece := dart, proper := heavy, admissible := trivial } }
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.heavyDartRun

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (problem V) HasEvenCycle)
  (dart : ctx.G.graph.Dart),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.2 = 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.degree_three_endpoint

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (problem V) HasEvenCycle)
  {source target : V},
  ctx.G.graph.Adj source target →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G source = 3 ∨
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G target = 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.invariant_I1

stages.title: Heavy-edge invariant
stages.summary: Every edge has an endpoint of degree exactly three in the audited minimal-counterexample context.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (problem V) HasEvenCycle)
  {source target : V},
  ctx.G.graph.Adj source target →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G source = 3 ∨
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G target = 3
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.invariant_I1

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (problem V) HasEvenCycle)
  (dart : ctx.G.graph.Dart),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.2 = 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.degree_three_endpoint

workflows.purpose: Inspect the proper-subgraph and single-edge CT2 audits in a hypothetical lexicographically minimal counterexample.

links.label: certified proper subgraph
links.description: The packed graph profile uses the same even-cycle target and checks one explicit proper-subgraph certificate without enumerating subgraphs.

links.label: generated CT2 profile
links.description: The graph support layer generates the deletion pieces, capability, and closure rule.

links.label: derives invariant

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (problem V) HasEvenCycle)
  (dart : ctx.G.graph.Dart),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.2 = 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.CT2Audit.degree_three_endpoint

links.description: The deletion-critical result rules out a dart with both endpoints heavier than degree three.

workflows.title: Parity-series CT3 compression

stages.title: Two-terminal series problem
stages.summary: Canonical path pieces are classified by their response to four finite gluing contexts.

primaryDeclaration.type: StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.seriesProblem

evidenceDeclarations.type: SeriesReduction.Piece → SeriesReduction.Context → SeriesReduction.SeriesObject
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.glue

evidenceDeclarations.type: SeriesReduction.Piece → SeriesReduction.Context → Bool
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.response

stages.title: CT3 response compression
stages.summary: CT3 computes the exact response vector and replaces a long path with its parity representative.

primaryDeclaration.type: (piece : SeriesReduction.Piece) → SeriesReduction.contract.AuditedRun SeriesReduction.branch piece
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.run

evidenceDeclarations.type: SeriesReduction.evenLongRun.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.Terminal.compression
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.evenLong_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type: SeriesReduction.oddLongRun.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.Terminal.compression
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.oddLong_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
SeriesReduction.evenLongRun.trace =
  [StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.vectorComputation,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionSearch, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.evenLong_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
SeriesReduction.oddCanonicalRun.trace =
  [StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.vectorComputation,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionSearch, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.tableValidation,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.rowLookup, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.knownRowTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.oddCanonical_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ (piece : SeriesReduction.Piece), StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.ValidTrace (SeriesReduction.run piece).trace
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.run_traceValid

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (piece : SeriesReduction.Piece),
  ∃ result,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT3.OutcomeClaim result.outcome ∧ StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.ValidTrace result.trace
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.run_total

stages.title: Target-preserving replacement
stages.summary: Equal response rows preserve the existence of an even cycle in every compatible context.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {left right : SeriesReduction.Piece},
  StructuralExhaustion.CT3.SameResponse SeriesReduction.ct3Spec left right →
    ∀ (context : SeriesReduction.Context),
      SeriesReduction.SeriesHasEvenCycle (SeriesReduction.glue left context) ↔
        SeriesReduction.SeriesHasEvenCycle (SeriesReduction.glue right context)
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.sameResponse_preserves_target

evidenceDeclarations.type:
StructuralExhaustion.CT3.SameResponse SeriesReduction.ct3Spec SeriesReduction.Piece.evenCanonical
  SeriesReduction.Piece.evenLong
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.even_series_sameResponse

evidenceDeclarations.type:
StructuralExhaustion.CT3.SameResponse SeriesReduction.ct3Spec SeriesReduction.Piece.oddCanonical
  SeriesReduction.Piece.oddLong
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.odd_series_sameResponse

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ (piece : SeriesReduction.Piece), StructuralExhaustion.CT3.OutcomeClaim (SeriesReduction.run piece).outcome
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.run_verified

stages.title: Literal packed replacement closure
stages.summary: The even-cycle package instantiates the same airtight literal-gluing CT3 stage as the Erdős proof.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∃ ctx,
  SeriesReduction.concretePackedInput.problem.rank ctx.G ≤
      SeriesReduction.concretePackedInput.problem.rank EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.concreteSeedObject✝ ∧
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.VerifiedStage
      SeriesReduction.concretePackedInput inferInstance ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.exists_duffinConcreteBoundariedReplacementStage

evidenceDeclarations.type: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.concretePackedInput

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) {replacement source : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T}
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  replacement.LexSmaller source →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries replacement outside).lexRank <
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries source outside).lexRank
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_lexRank_lt

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) [Nonempty T] (threshold : ℕ)
  (source replacement : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  (∀ (boundary : T),
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries replacement boundary =
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries source boundary) →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries threshold replacement →
      threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph source outside).minDegree →
        threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph replacement outside).minDegree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_preserves_minDegree

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  [inst : Nonempty T] (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.VerifiedStage input boundaries ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.verifiedStage

workflows.purpose: Inspect the independent Duffin-style series reduction and its four exact response coordinates.

links.label: instantiates CT3
links.description: The series contract supplies CT3's pieces, contexts, rows, response function, and work bound.

links.label: certified same response

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ (piece : SeriesReduction.Piece), StructuralExhaustion.CT3.OutcomeClaim (SeriesReduction.run piece).outcome
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.SeriesReduction.run_verified

links.description: The CT3 outcome exposes a response-equivalent representative used by the replacement theorem.

links.label: one certified replacement

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) →
      (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT3.CertifiedCompressionInput ctx) →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT3.CertifiedCompressionRun ctx input
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.runCertifiedCompression
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.impossible

links.description: Literal gluing derives target transport, baseline preservation, and strict whole-graph decrease from local replacement data.

workflows.title: Every edge is an induced P₂

stages.title: Graph with one selected edge
stages.summary: A Mathlib dart supplies two distinct adjacent vertices and therefore the canonical induced path on two vertices.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type uVertex} {G : SimpleGraph V} {left right : V},
  G.Adj left right → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HasInducedPath G 2
primaryDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.hasInducedPath_two_of_adj

evidenceDeclarations.type: {V : Type uVertex} → {G : SimpleGraph V} → {left right : V} → G.Adj left right → SimpleGraph.pathGraph 2 ↪g G
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.inducedPathTwoEmbeddingOfAdj

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type uVertex} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    object.graph.Dart → (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeProfile V).Code object
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeCertificate

stages.title: CT1 induced-path validation
stages.summary: The shared proof-carrying profile validates the induced P₂ embedding with one check and the exact C1 trace.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type uVertex} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    object.graph.Dart →
      StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedC1Run (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeProfile V).encoding.spec
        (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeInput object)
primaryDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type uVertex} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (dart : object.graph.Dart),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge object dart).result.terminal =
    StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.c1
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type uVertex} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (dart : object.graph.Dart),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge object dart).result.trace =
    [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.c1Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type uVertex} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (dart : object.graph.Dart),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HasInducedPath object.graph 2
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge_verified

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type uVertex} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (dart : object.graph.Dart),
  ∃ run,
    run.result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.c1 ∧
      run.result.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.c1Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge_total

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type uVertex} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (dart : object.graph.Dart),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge object dart).checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedC1Budget
          (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeProfile V).encoding.spec).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedC1Budget
                (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeProfile V).encoding.spec).size
            (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeInput object) +
          1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedC1Budget
            (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeProfile V).encoding.spec).degree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.runEdge_polynomial

evidenceDeclarations.type: InducedEdge.ConcreteK4.execution.result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.c1
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.InducedEdge.ConcreteK4.terminal_c1

evidenceDeclarations.type:
InducedEdge.ConcreteK4.execution.result.trace =
  [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.c1Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.InducedEdge.ConcreteK4.trace_exact

evidenceDeclarations.type: InducedEdge.ConcreteK4.execution.checks = 1
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.InducedEdge.ConcreteK4.one_check

workflows.purpose: Validate the textbook induced-edge fact through the same certificate-driven induced-path CT1 profile used by the Erdős P₁₃ stage.

links.label: validate induced embedding

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type uVertex} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    object.graph.Dart → (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeProfile V).Code object
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPath.edgeCertificate

links.description: The canonical embedding carried by the selected edge is passed directly to the shared certificate-driven CT1 runner.

workflows.title: Maximum matching by induced-P₂ packing

stages.title: Induced-edge supports
stages.summary: Each labelled induced P₂ embedding is one edge support; support-disjoint families are matchings.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT12.DisjointPacking.Profile V (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window object 2)
primaryDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.profile

evidenceDeclarations.type: {V : Type uVertex} → {G : SimpleGraph V} → {left right : V} → G.Adj left right → SimpleGraph.pathGraph 2 ↪g G
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.inducedPathTwoEmbeddingOfAdj

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (order : ℕ) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window object order → Finset V
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.support

stages.title: CT12 maximum matching audit
stages.summary: The core selects a maximum support-disjoint family and CT12 peels only that selected edge list with a vertex-linear bound.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
    (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
      (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P) →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult
          (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability P
            (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window object 2))
          ((StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.profile object).input context)
primaryDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V)
  (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run object context).terminal =
    StructuralExhaustion.CT12.Graph.Terminal.exhausted
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run_terminal_exhausted

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V)
  (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run object context).iterations ≤ FinEnum.card V
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run_iterations_le_vertices

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V)
  (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run object context).iterations =
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.matchingNumber object
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run_iterations_exact

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V)
  (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P),
  (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult.trace
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability P
          (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window object 2))
        (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run object context)).length ≤
    4 * FinEnum.card V + 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.run_trace_length_le_vertices

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V)
  (other : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.Matching object),
  (↑other).card ≤ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.matchingNumber object
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.maximum

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.remainderVertices object).card +
      2 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.matchingNumber object =
    FinEnum.card V
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.partition

evidenceDeclarations.type: MaximalMatching.ConcreteK4.execution.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT12.Graph.Terminal.exhausted
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.MaximalMatching.ConcreteK4.exhausted

evidenceDeclarations.type: MaximalMatching.ConcreteK4.execution.iterations ≤ FinEnum.card ConcreteK4.Vertex
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.MaximalMatching.ConcreteK4.bounded

stages.title: Edgeless unmatched remainder
stages.summary: Maximum implies maximal saturation, so the unmatched vertices contain no induced P₂ and hence no edge.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V)
  (left right : ↥(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.remainderVertices object)),
  ¬(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.remainder object).graph.Adj left right
primaryDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.remainder_no_edges

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (order : ℕ) (positive : 0 < order),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathFree
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.remainder object order positive).graph order
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.remainder_free

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V)
  (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.VerifiedStage object context
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.verifiedStage

workflows.purpose: Instantiate the reusable maximum disjoint-packing CT12 profile on the textbook family of graph edges.

links.label: instantiate disjoint packing
links.description: The graph layer supplies finite edge supports and one representative endpoint; the framework derives maximum selection and the CT12 schedule.

links.label: maximal saturation

evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteDisjointPacking.Profile.maximum_saturated

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (order : ℕ) (positive : 0 < order),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathFree
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.remainder object order positive).graph order
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.remainder_free

links.description: The selected maximum family intersects every other induced edge, making its vertex complement edgeless.

workflows.title: High-center deletion charge on K₃,₄

stages.title: Complete bipartite K₃,₄ profile
stages.summary: The three left vertices are exactly the degree-four assigned centers; every noncenter is one of the four degree-three right vertices.

primaryDeclaration.type: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HighCenterDeletionCharge.Profile ConcreteK34.object
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletionProfile

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ center ∈ ConcreteK34.leftCenters, 4 ≤ ConcreteK34.object.degree center
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK34.leftCenter_degree_at_least_four

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ vertex ∈ ConcreteK34.highCenterChargeProfile.core,
  vertex ∉ ConcreteK34.highCenterChargeProfile.assignedCenters → ConcreteK34.object.degree vertex = 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK34.nonleft_core_vertex_degree_eq_three

stages.title: Literal edgeless retained graph
stages.summary: Deleting the left part leaves exactly the four right vertices. Their induced graph is bottom, so internal minimum degree three is excluded directly.

primaryDeclaration.type: ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletionProfile.remainingObject.InternalMinDegreeFree 3
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK34.remainingObject_internalThreeCore_free

evidenceDeclarations.type: ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletionProfile.remainingCore = Finset.image (fun vertex => Sum.inr vertex) Finset.univ
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK34.remainingCore_eq_rightVertices

evidenceDeclarations.type: ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletionProfile.remainingObject.graph = ⊥
evidenceDeclarations.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK34.remainingObject_graph_eq_bot

stages.title: Choice-free receiver-overload bound
stages.summary: The same graph-owned theorem used by the Erdős Type B scope bounds negative net quarter-charge by twenty-one times assigned surplus plus the exact retained receiver overload.

primaryDeclaration.type:
-ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletionProfile.charge.netQuarterCharge ≤
  21 * ↑ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletionProfile.charge.assignedSurplus +
    ↑(ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletionProfile.receiverOverload ConcreteK34.remainingObject_internalThreeCore_free)
primaryDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.ConcreteK34.highCenterDeletion_deficit_bound

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V}
  (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HighCenterDeletionCharge.Profile object)
  (coreFree : profile.remainingObject.InternalMinDegreeFree 3),
  -profile.charge.netQuarterCharge ≤ 21 * ↑profile.charge.assignedSurplus + ↑(profile.receiverOverload coreFree)
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HighCenterDeletionCharge.Profile.neg_netQuarterCharge_le_twentyOne_mul_surplus_add_overload

workflows.purpose: Instantiate the reusable Type-B-to-Type-A charge transition on a textbook complete bipartite graph without using an external theorem.

links.label: delete all high centers
links.description: The profile's derived remaining support is exactly the right side of K₃,₄.

links.label: apply generic charge theorem
links.description: Direct internal-core freeness supplies the sole premise needed by the reusable receiver-overload inequality.

example.title: Even cycle
example.summary: A complete minimum-degree-three proof with a registered CT6→CT9 route, CT1 validation, and separate CT2, CT3, induced-path CT1, maximum-packing CT12, and high-center-deletion reuse workflows.

problemDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V → StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Capability (endpointParityProfile V).ct6Spec
problemDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.ct6Capability

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  (S : StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Spec P) →
    (capability : StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Capability S) →
      (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT6.Input P) → StructuralExhaustion.CT6.ExecutionResult S capability input
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT6.run

interfaceBindings.description: The endpoint-parity profile supplies the problem-specific CT6 failure predicate and branch enumeration.

problemDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (baseline : Baseline object) →
      StructuralExhaustion.Routes.CT6ToCT9.ItemCollectionAdapter
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT6.ActiveLedgerResidual (endpointParityProfile V).ct6Spec
          ((endpointParityProfile V).ct6Capability object) ((endpointParityProfile V).branchContext object baseline))
        ((endpointParityProfile V).ct9Capability object baseline).Item
problemDeclaration.name: EvenCycleExample.ct6ToCT9ItemAdapter

interfaceBindings.description: The item adapter gives the registered route the graph-specific endpoint-neighbor semantics required by CT9.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  (S : StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec P) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability S →
      (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Input P) → StructuralExhaustion.CT1.ExecutionResult S input
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.run

interfaceBindings.description: The graph target encoding turns an even-cycle certificate into CT1's finite realization interface.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
(input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) →
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target) →
    (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G) →
      (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value) →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionRun ctx (input.properSubgraphCT2Input ctx subgraph baseline)
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run

interfaceBindings.description: The even-cycle package instantiates the reusable packed minimum-degree cycle profile and its constant-work certified reduction runner.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) →
        (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Input capability ctx) →
          StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ExecutionResult capability ctx input
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.run

interfaceBindings.description: The generated deletion-only capability connects graph edge deletion to the generic CT2 runner.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  (S : StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Spec P) →
    (C : StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Capability S) →
      (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Input S) → StructuralExhaustion.CT3.ExecutionResult S C input
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.run

interfaceBindings.description: The series contract provides every problem-specific coordinate, response, admissibility, and complexity field consumed by CT3.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  [inst : Nonempty T] (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.VerifiedStage input boundaries ctx
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.verifiedStage

interfaceBindings.description: The two-terminal transfer consumes the exact literal-gluing graph profile used by the Erdős stage.
A selected edge supplies the concrete induced P₂ embedding; the graph profile provides the same CT1 encoding, runner, semantic theorem, trace, totality, and constant budget used for induced P₁₃.

problemDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT12.DisjointPacking.Profile V (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window object 2)
problemDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MaximumMatching.profile

frameworkDeclaration.type:
∀ {Vertex : Type uVertex} {Item : Type uItem} (profile : StructuralExhaustion.CT12.DisjointPacking.Profile Vertex Item)
  {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P),
  profile.VerifiedStage context
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.DisjointPacking.Profile.verifiedStage

interfaceBindings.description: Induced P₂ embeddings provide graph-specific supports; the same core maximum theorem and CT12 selected-list audit used by the Erdős P₁₃ packing provide the matching result.
